label: test_coverage_gap
summary: Core 429 rejection logic and sliding-window algorithm are correct. The diff fails on two acceptance criteria: (1) expired Map *entries* are never removed — only timestamps within an active entry are filtered — so IPs that stop sending requests leave stale Map entries that accumulate indefinitely (criterion 5 unmet, concrete OOM risk under churn); (2) the test suite covers only the under-limit happy path, omitting at-limit boundary, over-limit rejection, proxy header parsing, and cleanup — 5 of 6 required cases absent (criterion 6 unmet). Both defects are specific and fixable without redesign; the approach is sound.

findings.description: IP entries are never deleted from the Map — only timestamps within an active IP's array are filtered. IPs that stop sending requests keep their entry (and any remaining timestamps) in the Map forever, leaking memory without bound.
findings.suggestion: After filtering, check `if (recent.length === 0) hits.delete(ip)` before `hits.set(ip, recent)`, or add a periodic `setInterval` sweep that deletes entries whose filtered array is empty.

findings.description: Test file contains only 1 of the 6 required test cases (under-limit). Missing: at-limit boundary (exactly N requests pass), over-limit rejection (N+1 returns 429), X-Forwarded-For header parsing, and cleanup/expiry behavior.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for at-limit (N calls, all pass), over-limit (N+1 call returns 429 with correct JSON body), X-Forwarded-For (set header to '10.0.0.1, 192.168.1.1', verify 10.0.0.1 is the tracked key), and cleanup (advance mock clock past windowMs, verify Map entry removed or timestamp array emptied).

findings.description: The returned middleware function's `req`, `res`, and `next` parameters are untyped implicit `any`. In strict TypeScript projects this will fail or produce unsafe inference.
findings.suggestion: Type parameters with Express's `Request`, `Response`, `NextFunction` from `@types/express`, or define a minimal interface matching what the function actually accesses.

findings.description: X-Forwarded-For is client-controlled — any caller can spoof an arbitrary IP string to bypass the per-IP limit or poison the Map with arbitrary keys.
findings.suggestion: If this middleware is a security control, only trust X-Forwarded-For when `req.socket.remoteAddress` matches a known trusted proxy CIDR; otherwise fall through to `req.ip`.

fullReview.summary: Core 429 rejection logic and sliding-window algorithm are correct. The diff fails on two acceptance criteria: (1) expired Map *entries* are never removed — only timestamps within an active entry are filtered — so IPs that stop sending requests leave stale Map entries that accumulate indefinitely (criterion 5 unmet, concrete OOM risk under churn); (2) the test suite covers only the under-limit happy path, omitting at-limit boundary, over-limit rejection, proxy header parsing, and cleanup — 5 of 6 required cases absent (criterion 6 unmet). Both defects are specific and fixable without redesign; the approach is sound.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Middleware rejects with 429 when limit exceeded
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Line 14-16: `recent.push(now)` before the check means `recent.length > limit` triggers on the (limit+1)th request. Returns `res.status(429).json({ error: 'Too many requests' })` correctly.
Line 12: `timestamps.filter((t) => t > now - windowMs)` correctly implements a sliding window — only timestamps within the last `windowMs` milliseconds count.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Default limit: 100/minute, configurable via middleware options
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Lines 4-5: `opts.limit ?? 100` and `opts.windowMs ?? 60_000` provide correct defaults and allow caller override via `RateLimitOptions`.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Correctly handles X-Forwarded-For header when behind proxy
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Line 9: `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']?.split(',')[0]` extracts the first (leftmost/client) IP from the header, falling back to `req.ip`.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Cleans up expired entries to prevent memory leak
criteriaAssessment.evidence: The `filter` on line 12 removes old timestamps only for the IP currently making a request. IP keys for silent clients are never deleted from the `hits` Map. A high-churn scenario (many unique short-lived IPs) will grow the Map without bound.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Unit tests cover: under-limit, at-limit, over-limit, proxy header, cleanup
criteriaAssessment.evidence: tests/middleware/rateLimit.test.ts contains exactly one test ('allows requests under limit'). Tests for at-limit boundary, over-limit 429, X-Forwarded-For header, and cleanup/expiry are entirely absent.
